description:
Could a Chettinad mansion prove to be the blueprint for a modern-day home? Shaped with garnet Athangudi tiles, refurbished antiques and colour aplenty, this 2,400 sq ft home in Bengaluru by Studio Anvaya @studioanvaya skillfully remodels old-world grandeur for the 21st century.‚Ā†
‚Ā†
With Palakkad roots and a Tamil-Brahmin upbringing, the homeowners deeply admired the craftsmanship of their childhood dwellings. This strong tug of remembrance seeped into the imaginative framework they shared with the architect. Think breezy Thinnais (porches), carved colonnades, Belgian stained glass, lime-plastered walls and brass accents. The emanating result is a Chettinad-style home reimagined with a contemporary demeanour. Colour-speckled flooring, subtly shifting levels and heirlooms aplenty define the interiors, resulting in "a crossover between what the family has painstakingly held onto and newer additions, enabling the latter to become heirlooms for the future," concludes Ashwathy CK @ashwathyck, Principal Architect at Studio Anvaya.‚Ā†
‚Ā†
Tour the home from the link in our bio ūüĒó ‚Ā†
‚Ā†
Text by Lavanya Chopra @lavanyachopra‚Ā†
